APIKey.description: TrakRF API key (JWT). Format: "Bearer <jwt>". Mint keys from the API Keys section of your TrakRF account (key-management UI is tracked by TRA-393).

BearerAuth.description: Session JWT for internal endpoints (platform frontend uses this).
BearerAuth.name: Authorization

info.description: TrakRF public REST API. See docs.trakrf.id/api for the customer-facing reference.
info.title: TrakRF API

get.description: Return a paginated list of all assets in the organization, each with their associated tag identifiers.

202.description: Paginated list of assets with metadata

post.description:
Create a new asset record, optionally with one or more tag identifiers (RFID, BLE, barcode).
Returns the created asset with its assigned identifiers. The Location response header contains the canonical URL.

requestBody.description: Asset to create with optional identifiers

delete.description: Soft-delete an asset by its numeric ID. The asset is marked inactive and removed from future list results.

get.description: Fetch a single asset by its numeric ID, including all associated tag identifiers.

put.description: Update mutable fields on an existing asset. Only fields included in the request body are changed.

post.description:
Attach a tag identifier (RFID EPC, BLE beacon ID, barcode, etc.) to an existing asset.
The identifier must be unique within the organization.
post.summary: Add an identifier to an asset

delete.description: Detach a tag identifier from an asset by its identifier record ID.
delete.summary: Remove an identifier from an asset

get.description: Return a paginated list of all locations in the organization, each with their associated tag identifiers.

202.description: Paginated list of locations with metadata

post.description:
Create a new location in the hierarchy, optionally with one or more tag identifiers.
Set ParentLocationID to nest the location under an existing parent. The Location response header contains the canonical URL.

requestBody.description: Location to create with optional identifiers

delete.description: Soft-delete a location by its numeric ID. The location is marked inactive and removed from future list results.

get.description:
Fetch a single location by its numeric ID, including associated tag identifiers.
Pass include=relations to also receive the location's ancestors and immediate children in the response.

parameters.description: Pass 'relations' to include ancestors and children
parameters.name: include

put.description: Update mutable fields on an existing location. Only fields included in the request body are changed.

get.description: Return all ancestor locations from the root of the hierarchy down to the immediate parent of the specified location.
Return the immediate child locations of the specified location (one level deep only).
Return all descendant locations (children, grandchildren, etc.) beneath the specified location in the hierarchy.

post.description:
Attach a tag identifier (RFID EPC, BLE beacon ID, barcode, etc.) to an existing location.
The identifier must be unique within the organization.
post.summary: Add an identifier to a location

delete.description: Detach a tag identifier from a location by its identifier record ID.
delete.summary: Remove an identifier from a location

get.description:
Return a paginated timeline of where an asset has been scanned, with duration-at-location calculations.
Defaults to the last 30 days when no date range is supplied. Dates must be RFC 3339 (e.g. 2026-01-15T00:00:00Z).

parameters.description: Include scans on or after this time (RFC 3339)
parameters.name: start_date

parameters.description: Include scans on or before this time (RFC 3339)
parameters.name: end_date

get.description:
Return a paginated snapshot of every asset paired with its most recent scan location.
Optionally filter by a specific location or search by asset name/identifier.

parameters.description: Filter to assets last seen at this location ID
parameters.name: location_id

parameters.description: Full-text match on asset name or identifier
parameters.name: search
